name: ​We Are in a Soup
description:
When did you last cook together?

​We Are in a Soup is a participatory workshop that uses cooking and making as ways to return to presence, relation, and care. In a time shaped by speed, crisis, and emotional numbness, the workshop invites participants to slow down through simple, shared acts. Each participant brings a vegetable, and together they prepare a single pot of soup, chopping, stirring, and serving it collectively.

Alongside this, participants shape clay bowls by hand, creating vessels that hold touch, time, and memory. Cooking, serving, and cleaning are shared equally, shifting the focus from consumption to participation. Food becomes more than nourishment; it becomes a way of being together. This workshop creates a temporary space for warmth, attention, and collective presence, where care is practised rather than performed.

​This walk explores the themes of ‘Cook’ & ‘Eat’ in relation to Science Gallery Bengaluru’s Exhibition CALORIE, the breakdown.

name: Benda-Cal-ooru : Calorie meets Bengaluru
description:
What’s your favourite pastime in Bengaluru? Is it going to your local dharshini for thindi, or cafe hopping in the
evening? Or do you complain about the traffic and just order in? 

One thing is for sure, the city has much to offer for food. But if there’s so much consumption, what of the production? How does Bengaluru’s history and its institutions contribute to this culture? Come for an evening of play and exploration of Bengaluru’s history,institutions and their connection to food!
Join our Experimentors Megha and Tejashree for their walkthrough, to experience Bengaluru Kathe Calorie Jothe.

name: Tasting the Landscape
description:
When does food become a pest, and who decides?

Some cultures view grasshoppers and locusts (Cavallette in Italian) as symbols of plague and famine, while others consume them as an everyday protein source. Join artist Giulia Berra for a deep dive into her exhibit ‘Cavallette (Grasshoppers),’ as she unpacks connections between the apparent extremes of food and famine and people and pests. She will explore her artistic method based on natural leftovers and residual elements, focusing on artworks made from insects or food waste. This will be followed by a virtual table where participants can share their experiences, as people do at dinner.

name: Walk Into a Fragmented Forest: Opening Event
description:
How do you turn years of forest data into something you can walk through?

Join artists Shreni Sanghvi and Abhishek Kapahi for a walkthrough of their exhibit, Walk Into a Fragmented Forest, followed by talks about their collaboration with ecologist Meghna Krishnadas's lab at the National Centre for Biological Sciences. The collaboration draws on seedling census records, biochemistry, and field data from the Western Ghats to build a room of light, sand, and sound. Through their work, they reexamine ecological interactions through the lens of art. Post the talk, historian of science Jahnavi Phalkey and curator Ravi Agarwal will offer their reflections on the work and the need for interdisciplinary practice.

The work is an outcome of the Entangled Ecosystems Grant by Science Gallery Bengaluru and Shared Ecologies, an annual programme that brings art and ecology into conversation

name: Fermentation Table: A Supper Club
description:
How can fermentation become a part of everyday cooking?

'Fermentation Table' is an immersive culinary experience featuring a 5-course fermentation-inspired meal, an interactive fermentation exhibit, and practical tips to help you start fermenting at home. 

Created by Chef Erika in collaboration with Scoby Labs, this experience will explore the science of fermentation and its role as a low-waste cooking technique, challenging you to use parts of vegetables that are often overlooked.

Showcasing the health benefits and environmental impact of prolonging the life of ingredients that might otherwise go wasted, this event will introduce participants to new depths of flavours, rethinking how fermentation can become part of your everyday cooking.

name: Portraits of a Fragmented Forest
description:
How do you translate ecological data into a portrait?

The exhibition ‘Walk Into a Fragmented Forest’ draws on seedling census records, biochemistry, and field data from the Western Ghats to build a room of light, soil, and sound. In this workshop, participants collectively construct a portrait of a forest using ecological data from the fragmented Kadamane forest. 

Join artists Shreni Sanghvi and Abhishek Kapahi as you create a unique hand-made portrait and sonic signature of a species, moving from observation to data to expression. The workshop culminates in a wall of fragmented portraits, connected through lines of chemical kinship.

name:
Educator Workshop: 
Interdisciplinary Thinking In Practice
description:
Click here to [register]! 

Learning is often presented as distinct, neatly defined subjects.
Interdisciplinary thinking integrates them, opening up a word that is complex, interconnected, and full of surprises!

The Educator Workshop is a full-day immersive workshop for educators from across the city, to explore new ideas, think across disciplines, and reimagine learning together. Because the future of education is becoming increasingly interdisciplinary.

What to expect?
> Experience our exhibition-season CALORIE, where ideas from science, art, design, and society intersect.
> Unpack interdisciplinarity through hands-on activities and reflective discussions.
> Explore NCERT topics through a fresh lens.
> Collaborate in small groups to design practical ideas for sparking interdisciplinarity in the classroom. 
> Present your insights in a lively pitching session.

Who is this for?
Educators and teachers across subjects (Classes 7–12) who value curiosity, inquiry, and exploring ideas across disciplines.


Come, be a learner for a day.
Connect with fellow educators.
Return inspired—with practical tools and renewed energy for teaching.

Click here to [register]! 

[register]: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdbACchT1wSVyhPTilwpQG84SMcXM2kEq2hbpp2t9xjj5XoMg/viewform

name: The Jazz Kitchen: Jazz, Poetry & Songwriting
description:
What happens when food, poetry and jazz meet?

Jazz poetry is a literary genre in which poetry is informed by jazz music. That is, the poet responds to and writes about jazz. It encompasses a variety of forms, rhythms, and sounds. But writing about jazz poetry, as they say, is like dancing about architecture. This session explores that paradox as the MoonArra quartet performs poetry with jazz's rhythmic and improvisational techniques. Participants can engage in the process of creating a basic blues song on the theme of food, which will be set to rhythm and accompaniment by the quartet.

name: Bites of Design: Shaping How We Consume
description:
A menu, a market aisle, a fancy plate of food.
Ever noticed how some things stand out instantly, while others fade into the background?
Here's where design comes into the picture. It quietly nudges what we notice, what we pick, and what we leave behind.
Join  our Mediators Shradhitha S and Manjuvarshini as we walk through the CALORIE exhibition at Science Gallery Bengaluru, spotting design in unexpected places.
Once you see it, you can't unsee it!
